name: ExtensionClinicalImpression_Finding_Item
title: R5: What was found additional types
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ClinicalImpression.finding.item` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ClinicalImpression.finding.item` 0..1 `CodeableReference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Condition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Observation,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DocumentReference)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ClinicalImpression.finding.item` 0..1 `CodeableReference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Condition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Observation,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DocumentReference)`
*  R4B: `ClinicalImpression.finding.itemCodeableConcept` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`
*  R4: `ClinicalImpression.finding.itemCodeableConcept` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ClinicalImpression.finding.item` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ClinicalImpression.finding.itemCodeableConcept` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
Element `ClinicalImpression.finding.item` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ClinicalImpression.finding.itemReference` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `ClinicalImpression.finding.item` do not cover the following types: CodeableReference.

element.short: R5: What was found additional types

The mappings for `ClinicalImpression.finding.item` do not cover the following types: CodeableReference.

element.short: DataType slice for a FHIR R5 `CodeableReference` value
element.definition: Slice to indicate the presence of a R5 `CodeableReference` in FHIR R4

element.short: R5: Reference to a concept (by class) (new)
element.comment:
Element `CodeableReference.concept` is part of an existing definition because parent element `CodeableReference` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `CodeableReference.concept` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `CodeableReference` is not mapped.
Element `CodeableReference.concept` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.definition: A reference to a concept - e.g. the information is identified by its general class to the degree of precision found in the terminology.
